title: Canary Deployment with Traffic Split
description:
Implement a complete canary deployment pattern with controlled traffic distribution between stable and canary versions using replica count ratios.

**Focus**: Multi-deployment coordination, service selector design for traffic splitting, and gradual traffic shifting through replica count management.

This advanced exercise demonstrates how to safely roll out new versions by sending a small percentage of traffic (20%) to a canary deployment while keeping most traffic on the stable version (80%). The critical insight is that the Service selector must only include common labels (`app: webapp`), NOT version-specific labels (`track: stable/canary`), to route traffic to both deployments. Traffic naturally splits based on pod count ratio: 8 stable pods + 2 canary pods = 80/20 distribution via round-robin load balancing. This pattern minimizes blast radius - if the canary has bugs, only 20% of users are affected, and you can instantly rollback by deleting the canary deployment.
steps: Configure both with app: webapp label plus unique track labels
Create Service that selects ONLY app: webapp (not track or version)
Test scaling canary to increase traffic percentage
hints: Traffic split is determined by replica count ratio (8:2 = 80:20)
Service selector must NOT include track or version labels
Both deployments need app: webapp for service routing
Use track: stable and track: canary to distinguish deployments

editor_config.initial_code:
# Stable Deployment (80% of traffic)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp-stable
  labels:
    app: webapp
    track: stable
spec:
  replicas: # TODO: How many replicas for 80%? (hint: 8 of 10 total)
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp
      track: stable
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: webapp
        track: stable
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: webapp
        image: # TODO: nginx:1.26-alpine for stable
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80

---
# Canary Deployment (20% of traffic)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp-canary
  labels:
    app: webapp
    track: canary
spec:
  replicas: # TODO: How many replicas for 20%? (hint: 2 of 10 total)
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp
      track: canary
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: webapp
        track: canary
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: webapp
        image: # TODO: nginx:1.27-alpine for canary
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80

---
# Service — selects BOTH deployments via common label only
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: webapp
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
  selector:
    app: webapp   # ⚠️ Only app label — NOT track. Both deployments match.

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp-stable
spec:
  replicas: 8  # 80% of total capacity (8 out of 10 pods)
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp
      track: stable
      version: "1.26"
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: webapp      # Common label for service routing
        track: stable    # Distinguishes from canary
        version: "1.26"  # Specific version identifier
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: webapp
        image: nginx:1.26-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
          name: http
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp-canary
spec:
  replicas: 2  # 20% of total capacity (2 out of 10 pods)
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp
      track: canary
      version: "1.27"
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: webapp      # Common label for service routing
        track: canary    # Distinguishes from stable
        version: "1.27"  # Specific version identifier
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: webapp
        image: nginx:1.27-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
          name: http
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: webapp
  labels:
    app: webapp
spec:
  type: NodePort  # Minikube-compatible service type
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
    name: http
  selector:
    app: webapp  # ✅ CRITICAL: Only common label, no track/version
    # This allows service to route to BOTH stable and canary pods
    # Traffic splits naturally based on replica count (8:2 = 80:20)
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Apply all manifests
kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for both deployments to be ready
timeout=120
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  stable_ready=$(kubectl get deployment webapp-stable -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  canary_ready=$(kubectl get deployment webapp-canary -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  
  if [ "$stable_ready" = "8" ] && [ "$canary_ready" = "2" ]; then
    echo "✓ Both deployments ready (stable: 8, canary: 2)"
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done

# Verify service endpoints include both deployments
endpoint_count=$(kubectl get endpoints webapp -o jsonpath='{.subsets[*].addresses[*].targetRef.name}' | wc -w)

if [ "$endpoint_count" -eq "10" ]; then
  echo "✓ Service has 10 endpoints (8 stable + 2 canary)"
  
  # Verify service selector is correct (only app label)
  selector=$(kubectl get svc webapp -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector}')
  
  if echo "$selector" | grep -q '"app":"webapp"' && ! echo "$selector" | grep -q "track"; then
    echo "✓ Service selector correctly configured (app only, no track/version)"
    
    # Verify labels on deployments
    stable_track=$(kubectl get deployment webapp-stable -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.metadata.labels.track}')
    canary_track=$(kubectl get deployment webapp-canary -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.metadata.labels.track}')
    
    if [ "$stable_track" = "stable" ] && [ "$canary_track" = "canary" ]; then
      echo "✓ Deployments have correct track labels"
      exit 0
    fi
  else
    echo "✗ Service selector includes track/version labels (will route to only one deployment)"
    exit 1
  fi
fi

echo "✗ Canary deployment validation failed"
kubectl get deployments -l app=webapp
kubectl get svc webapp -o yaml | grep -A 3 "selector:"
exit 1
